mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer confidence and economic momentum, driving sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy over 60-day horizons, with a Sharpe ratio below 0.5 across all backtest periods.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average indicates manufacturing weakness that suppresses corporate revenue expectations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of observations when INDPRO crosses below its 12-month average.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid month-over-month increase in the CPI-U exceeding 0.3 percentage points triggers immediate inflation hedging and rate hike expectations, causing sharp negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are non-negative in more than 50% of high-inflation months, or the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 0.5pp.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields a negative cumulative 60-day return in more than 40% of triggered periods, or Rank IC drops below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate profit margins and earnings forecasts, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 prices over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 revisions show zero correlation with 20-day forward equity returns, or the signal generates negative alpha after transaction costs.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production coinciding with stable monetary policy indicates supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins and trigger immediate negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 1.0% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS remains unchanged
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in over 55% of cases when industrial production drops sharply without rate adjustments, invalidating the supply shock transmission channel.
